name: Open Source AI Summit 2026
summary: The flagship conference for open-weights model builders, framework maintainers, and OSS adopters.
description: Three days of deep technical talks, hands-on workshops, and community-led unconference sessions. Topics span training, inference, evaluation, safety, and policy for open AI.

name: NeurIPS 2026
summary: The premier machine learning research conference.
description: Workshops, tutorials, posters, and main-track papers covering the full breadth of ML research. Multiple open-source AI workshops track in parallel.

name: LLM Inference Meetup — July 2026
summary: Monthly virtual meetup focused on production LLM inference on open models.
description: Lightning talks from llama.cpp, vLLM, and SGLang maintainers, followed by an open Q&A. Free to attend.

name: EuraLi Open AI Conference
summary: European conference covering open models, regulation, and AI sovereignty.
description: Two days of keynotes and panel discussions plus a third hands-on day for builders. Streamed live for remote attendees.

name: Open Agents Hackathon 2026
summary: 48-hour global hackathon building production agents on open-source frameworks.
description: Form a team of up to four, pick an open agent framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenHands), and ship a working demo. Top projects get featured in our monthly newsletter.

name: MLOps World 2026
summary: The MLOps community's annual gathering, with a strong open-source track.
description: Practitioner-focused talks on training pipelines, evaluation infrastructure, and production deployment of open-weight models.

name: AI Safety Unconference 2026
summary: Community-run unconference on open, reproducible AI safety research.
description: Attendees propose and host sessions on alignment, interpretability, evaluations, and red-teaming for open models. Format inspired by BarCamps.
